statusLine: EU fuel security is under strain with 19 of 27 countries in critical status across fuel types despite adequate overall regional reserves.
fullAnalysis:
The EU's fuel security position presents a mixed picture in March 2026, with regional averages above the 90-day mandatory minimum but significant disparities between member states. While EU-wide reserves show 96.3 days for petrol, 88.8 days for diesel, and 87.3 days for jet fuel, the distribution reveals critical vulnerabilities in multiple countries.

A concerning 19 of 27 EU countries face critical fuel shortages in at least one category, with Luxembourg, Poland, Austria, Romania, Portugal, and Italy experiencing critical levels across all three fuel types. Diesel supplies appear most constrained, with 15 countries below critical thresholds, while jet fuel shortages affect 16 countries. The situation reflects recent strategic reserve releases following Middle East supply disruptions.

Fuel prices have risen substantially, with Brent crude climbing 35.9% since late February to $98.5/barrel, driving EU petrol prices to €1.851/L and diesel to €1.87/L. Northern European countries face the highest costs, with Denmark reaching €2.50/L for petrol. Eastern European nations including Poland and Romania combine both low reserves and relatively affordable prices, suggesting potential supply chain pressures rather than market-driven shortages.
keyPoints: 19 of 27 EU countries face critical fuel shortages in at least one category, with 6 countries critical across all fuel types
Diesel supplies most affected with 15 countries below critical levels, followed by jet fuel with 16 countries in critical status
Oil prices have surged 35.9% since late February, with Brent crude reaching $98.5/barrel and EU petrol averaging €1.851/L
Eastern European countries including Luxembourg, Poland, Austria, and Romania show the most severe multi-fuel shortages
Despite country-level concerns, EU regional averages remain above 90-day mandatory minimums for petrol (96.3 days) though diesel (88.8 days) and jet fuel (87.3 days) are closer to thresholds
